This article about a Brazilian journalist is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Victorino_Chermont&action=edit).
(24 April 1973 in Rio de Janeiro – 28 November 2016 in La Unión) was a Brazilian [sports journalist](/wiki/Sports_journalist "Sports journalist") and television presenter.

["Jornalistas do Brasil inteiro acompanhavam a Chapecoense"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161130043435/http://www.correiodopovo.com.br/Esportes/Futebol/2016/11/604184/Jornalistas-do-Brasil-inteiro-acompanhavam-a-Chapecoense). Archived from [the original](http://www.correiodopovo.com.br/Esportes/Futebol/2016/11/604184/Jornalistas-do-Brasil-inteiro-acompanhavam-a-Chapecoense) on 30 November 2016.

["Voo da Chapecoense levava mais de 20 jornalistas brasileiros; 6 eram do Fox Sports"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161130044248/http://portalimprensa.com.br/noticias/ultimas_noticias/78732/voo+da+chapecoense+levava+mais+de+20+jornalistas+brasileiros). Archived from [the original](http://portalimprensa.com.br/noticias/ultimas_noticias/78732/voo+da+chapecoense+levava+mais+de+20+jornalistas+brasileiros) on 30 November 2016.

["Fox Sports tira Victorino Chermont do SporTV"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161130051254/http://www.portalmidiaesporte.com/2012/01/fox-sports-tira-victorino-chermont-do.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.portalmidiaesporte.com/2012/01/fox-sports-tira-victorino-chermont-do.html) on 30 November 2016.

["Renato Maurício Prado estreia hoje novo programa no Fox Sports"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161130112408/http://natelinha.uol.com.br/noticias/2013/06/03/renato-mauricio-prado-estreia-hoje-novo-programa-no-fox-sports-62088.php). Archived from [the original](http://natelinha.uol.com.br/noticias/2013/06/03/renato-mauricio-prado-estreia-hoje-novo-programa-no-fox-sports-62088.php) on 30 November 2016.

["1º Prêmio Botequim Cultural – Vencedores"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161130042252/http://botequimcultural.com.br/1o-premio-botequim-cultural-vencedores/). Archived from [the original](http://botequimcultural.com.br/1o-premio-botequim-cultural-vencedores/) on 30 November 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Brazilian sports journalist (1973–2016)

| Victorino Chermont | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1973-04-24)24 April 1973  [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil |
| Died | 28 November 2016(2016-11-28) (aged 43)  [La Unión](/wiki/La_Uni%C3%B3n,_Antioquia "La Unión, Antioquia"), Colombia |
| Occupation | [Journalist](/wiki/Journalist "Journalist") |

**Victorino Chermont** (24 April 1973 in Rio de Janeiro – 28 November 2016 in La Unión) was a Brazilian [sports journalist](/wiki/Sports_journalist "Sports journalist") and television presenter.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victorino_Chermont&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Victorino Chermont worked for [Rede Bandeirantes](/wiki/Rede_Bandeirantes "Rede Bandeirantes") in [Rádio Globo](/wiki/R%C3%A1dio_Globo "Rádio Globo"), [SporTV](/wiki/SporTV "SporTV")[[1]](#cite_note-1) and [Fox Sports Brazil](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Brazil) "Fox Sports (Brazil)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) On this channel he presented *A Última Palavra* (The Final Saying).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He received several awards including the Botequim Prize for best sports journalist in 2012.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Chermont died in the crash of [LaMia Airlines Flight 2933](/wiki/LaMia_Airlines_Flight_2933 "LaMia Airlines Flight 2933") on 28 November 2016 while covering the [Associação Chapecoense de Futebol](/wiki/Associa%C3%A7%C3%A3o_Chapecoense_de_Futebol "Associação Chapecoense de Futebol") team for [Fox Sports](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Brazil) "Fox Sports (Brazil)").
